response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and some low-latency DLang systems work, but the role requires deep hardware-focused expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears anywhere in the resume. While the years-of-experience bar is met, the domain mismatch is fundamental for a principal-level hardware performance architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Some C/C++ and embedded/hardware-adjacent exposure (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms, audio DSP thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No deep learning, AI accelerator, or GPU workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture experience
